title: aster umbellatus subsp. pubens
intro: Aster umbellatus subsp. pubens, also known as the wood's aster or small white aster, is a perennial plant species native to eastern North America. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant activities. The plant is believed to offer several health benefits, such as reducing fever, alleviating pain, and supporting the immune system. Aster umbellatus subsp. pubens can be prepared for herbal use through infusions, teas, and tinctures, although further research is necessary to confirm its efficacy and safety as a medicinal plant.
